preparation_method: sweetened with rock sugar or honey (optional)
main_ingredients: American ginseng root fibres (洋参须, Panax quinquefolius rootlets)
dried chrysanthemum flowers (菊花, Chrysanthemum morifolium)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 250 ml (250 g) of cold ginseng chrysanthemum tea, as typically served in a cup or glass at a Singaporean Chinese restaurant or hawker stall.

nutrient_source.title: Chrysanthemum tea, brewed — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; supplemented with peer-reviewed phytochemical data for American ginseng infusion
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA or HPB entry exists for ginseng chrysanthemum tea as a combined beverage. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central data for brewed herbal/chrysanthemum tea (essentially hot water infusion of dried flowers and plant material), which is nutritionally near-identical to plain brewed herbal tea: negligible macronutrients, trace minerals, and minimal sodium. American ginseng rootlet infusion at typical beverage concentrations contributes negligible additional macronutrients per 100 g of beverage. Rock sugar, if added, would increase carbohydrate and energy; values here represent the unsweetened or lightly sweetened base beverage as a conservative estimate consistent with USDA herbal tea data. Energy ~2 kcal/100 g, protein ~0 g, fat ~0 g, carbohydrate ~0.4 g per 100 g based on USDA brewed herbal tea reference.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of beverages — herbal tea and low-carbohydrate infusions; University of Sydney GI Database reference for non-caloric and near-zero-carbohydrate beverages
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for ginseng chrysanthemum tea in the University of Sydney GI Database. This beverage contains approximately 0.4 g carbohydrate per 100 g (1.0 g per 250 ml serving), which is below the threshold for a meaningful glycemic response. By convention for essentially zero-carbohydrate beverages (plain tea, herbal infusions), glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value, effectively zero) and gi_category is 'low'. Glycemic load per 250 ml serving = (1 × 1.0) / 100 = 0.01, rounded to 0.0. This approach is consistent with University of Sydney GI Database conventions for non-glycemic beverages.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~2 mg per 100 g; ~5 mg per 250 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: Ginseng chrysanthemum tea is very low in sodium (~5 mg per serving) and is generally considered safe and potentially beneficial for individuals with hypertension; chrysanthemum has been used in traditional Chinese medicine as a mild hypotensive herb, though clinical evidence remains limited and patients on antihypertensive medications should consult their physician before consuming large quantities.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate content and very low glycemic load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Ginseng chrysanthemum tea contains approximately 0.4 g carbohydrate per 100 g and has a glycemic load of effectively zero per 250 ml serving, making it suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; American ginseng (Panax quinquefolius) has been studied in peer-reviewed trials for modest postprandial glucose-lowering effects, though the concentration in a typical beverage infusion is low and should not replace prescribed diabetes management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero cholesterol, and antioxidant flavonoids from chrysanthemum flowers
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Ginseng chrysanthemum tea contains no fat, no cholesterol, and negligible sodium per serving, and chrysanthemum flowers provide luteolin and apigenin flavonoids with antioxidant properties that may support cardiovascular health; individuals with cardiovascular disease can generally include this beverage as part of a balanced diet, though sweetened versions with added rock sugar should be consumed in moderation to avoid excess caloric intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ginseng Chrysanthemum Tea (洋参须菊花茶, Yáng shēn xū jú huā chá) is a traditional Chinese herbal beverage widely consumed in Singapore and across the Chinese diaspora. It is prepared by steeping dried American ginseng rootlets (洋参须, Panax quinquefolius fibrous roots) and dried chrysanthemum flowers (菊花, Chrysanthemum morifolium) in hot water, then typically served cold as a refreshing herbal drink. The beverage has a delicate floral aroma from the chrysanthemum blossoms, a mildly bitter and slightly sweet herbal taste from the ginseng rootlets, and a light golden-yellow colour. Rock sugar or honey is sometimes added for sweetness. In traditional Chinese medicine (TCM), this tea is valued for its perceived cooling (yin-nourishing) properties, believed to reduce internal heat (上火, shàng huǒ), soothe the eyes, and support general wellbeing. Nutritionally, ginseng chrysanthemum tea is essentially a near-zero-calorie beverage: approximately 2 kcal per 100 g, with negligible protein (0 g), negligible fat (0 g), and very low carbohydrate (~0.4 g per 100 g). It contains trace minerals including potassium (~21 mg/100 g), calcium (~2 mg/100 g), and magnesium (~2 mg/100 g). The glycemic index is effectively zero (set to 1 by convention for non-glycemic beverages) and the glycemic load per 250 ml serving is 0.0, making it highly suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The beverage is naturally vegan, gluten-free, dairy-free, and halal. It is commonly served cold at Chinese restaurants, hot pot establishments, and herbal tea stalls in Singapore, and is available on food delivery platforms such as GrabFood.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for ginseng chrysanthemum tea exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central as a combined beverage product. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central brewed herbal tea data, which is the closest approved source match. The beverage is nutritionally near-identical to plain brewed herbal tea at typical preparation concentrations. GI is estimated by convention for near-zero-carbohydrate beverages. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. protein_g is naturally zero: this is a water-based herbal infusion containing no protein-bearing ingredients at beverage concentration; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central brewed herbal tea reference. 2. fat_g is naturally zero: herbal tea infusions contain no fat; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 3. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are naturally zero for the same reason. 4. sugar_g is naturally zero in the unsweetened base preparation; if rock sugar is added, sugar content increases significantly. 5. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as the plant solids are not consumed in a strained beverage. 6. cholesterol_mg is naturally zero; plant-based beverage. 7. vitamin_b12_mcg is naturally zero; plant-based beverage with no animal-derived ingredients. 8. vitamin_d_mcg is naturally zero; no dietary vitamin D source present. 9. vitamin_c_mg is confirmed zero per USDA brewed herbal tea reference at typical preparation. 10. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA brewed herbal tea reference used. 11. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source reference. 12. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source reference. 13. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source reference. 14. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source reference. 15. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source reference. 16. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source reference. 17. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source reference. 18. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source reference. 19. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates at meaningful levels; gi_category low per convention for near-zero-carbohydrate herbal infusions. 20. Served cold (冷) as indicated in the input record from Hai Kah Lang, Funan Mall, GrabFood Singapore. 21. Price SGD 5.67 per serving as recorded from GrabFood Singapore source.
